description.value:
Dwarves

Stereotype: Greedy as a dragon, stubborn as a glacier.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Stability: 0

f8b6RNOMHuNHgNS8.name: Purchased Traits
f8b6RNOMHuNHgNS8.description: You have 3 ancestry points to spend on the following traits.

description.value: You can paint a rune onto your skin (10 min work). Only one active at a time. * *Detection:* Glows near chosen creature/object type (20 sq). * *Light:* Sheds light (10 sq). Toggle as maneuver. * *Voice:* Rune-echo bond with one willing creature (1 mile; functions as a telepathic bond).
You cannot be made *weakened*.
Reshape unworked stone with song (1 hour ritual).
+1 Stability.

A bog-iron and peat community built on stilts above hungry ground, with seasonal movement between safe platforms and hard-learned routes.

Language: MyrmÃ¥l

Environment: Rural; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Labor

Skills: Crafting, plus two more skills from the crafting or lore skill groups (Quick Build: Crafting, History, Blacksmithing).

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 200

Perk: *Workmanâ€™s Shortcut*. Once per respite, reduce the time of one mundane crafting or repair project by half.
Whenever you make a test to craft something and donâ€™t have a skill that applies to the test, you gain a +1 bonus to the power roll.

Air for movement. Earth for permanence. Fire for destruction. Water for change. Green for growth. Rot for death. Void for the mysteryâ€” that which cannot be known. Years of study and practice and poring over tomes brought you the revelations that allow you to manipulate these building blocks of reality. Now you use your mastery of the seven elements to destroy, create, and warp the world with magic. As an elementalist, you can unleash your wrath across a field of foes, debilitate enemies, ward yourself and allies, manipulate terrain, warp space, and more. Your elemental specialization determines which of these talents you excel at.

O5zNZukudl8zmdj7.description: You gain one skill of your choice.

OO4J9JEganSdPot0.description: Choose an elemental specialization from the following options. (Quick Build: Fire.)

I2Uc8NTZIkyrcibE.name: Enchantment
I2Uc8NTZIkyrcibE.description: Choose an enchantment from the following options. (Quick Build: Enchantment of Destruction.}

BXRvqQ4O20Gy5jJi.name: Elementalist Ward
BXRvqQ4O20Gy5jJi.description: You create an invisible elemental ward that protects you. Choose one of the following wards. (Quick Build: Ward of Surprising Reactivity.)

BDC4sLfZqYeQIsnU.name: Signature Abilities
BDC4sLfZqYeQIsnU.description: Choose two signature abilities from the following options. Signature abilities can be used at will. (Quick Build: Bifurcated Incineration, Viscous Fire.)

frZU6nF70JOoCpxZ.name: 3 Essence Abilities
frZU6nF70JOoCpxZ.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 3 essence to use. (Quick Build: The Flesh, a Crucible.)

phkgdOT8vYWnJXbz.name: 5 Essence Abilities
phkgdOT8vYWnJXbz.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 5 essence to use. (Quick Build: Conflagration.)

obJZO7tdAOXGnoyW.name: Perk
obJZO7tdAOXGnoyW.description: You gain one crafting, lore, or supernatural perk of your choice.

NyffE8PhSBk2tmP8.name: New 5-Essence Ability
NyffE8PhSBk2tmP8.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 5 essence to use. Alternatively, you can choose one of the 5-essence abilities you didnâ€™t select at 1st level

3s9BqdXOieneBjhR.name: 7 Essence Abilities
3s9BqdXOieneBjhR.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 7 essence to use.Â

qGtr9Vq2BZq7zDi5.description: Your Reason score increases to 3. Additionally, you can increase one of your characteristic scores by 1, to a maximum of 3.

K7o3iqZKLnhQ0tKR.description: Each of your characteristic scores increases by 1, to a maximum of 4.

QATlAcA9aHkGyQUt.description: Your Reason score increases to 5. Additionally, you can increase one of your characteristic scores by 1, to a maximum of 5.

JvmbhstZHzv0snJg.name: Perk
JvmbhstZHzv0snJg.description: You gain one perk of your choice.

Qq9C4BJWThK1RTD0.description: You gain one crafting, lore, or supernatural perk of your choice.

KVQsFKQKE5uuhltN.description: You gain one perk of your choice.

sC3UMDKZ21nFr7Zz.name: Perk
sC3UMDKZ21nFr7Zz.description: You gain one crafting, lore, or supernatural perk of your choice.

In9yA4GklXRlhk10.name: 9 Essence Ability
In9yA4GklXRlhk10.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 9 essence to use.

Pqn0ZzCTZOsv1l1o.name: Wyrding
Pqn0ZzCTZOsv1l1o.description: You can spend 10 uninterrupted minutes to create a freeform magic spell for a variety of situations.

1UMLlKp8i6Vq9lbP.name: 9 Essence Ability
1UMLlKp8i6Vq9lbP.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 9 essence to use. Alternatively, you can choose one of the 9-assence abilities you didnâ€™t select at 5th level

lSdZywD8Z9nYl1cR.name: 11 Essence Ability
lSdZywD8Z9nYl1cR.description: Choose one heroic ability from the following options, each of which costs 11 essence to use.

BV9p2TYBgtDcknte.name: Grand Wyrding
BV9p2TYBgtDcknte.description: You have mastered the magic of shaping a wyrd, and can use your Wyrding feature as a main action. Additionally, when you have 5 or more Victories, â€¦

system.story: You cast a ball of elemental energy at a foe.

effect.after: When you make this strike, choose the damage type from one of the following options: acid, cold, corruption, fire, lightning, poison, or sonic.

system.story: Your mastery of elemental power lets you customize your conjurations.

effect.before:
Choose one of the following effects:

  • You use the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.emug9cXuwndDrWzu]{Knockback} maneuver, but its distance becomes the range of your Hurl Element ability, and you use Reason instead of Might for the power roll.

  • You choose a creature within the distance of your Hurl Element ability and one of the following damage types: [[/damage @R type=acid/cold/corruption/fire/lightning/poison/sonic]]. That creature takes damage of the chosen type equal to your Reason score.

  • You teleport up to a number of squares equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}. If you choose this option, you can spend essence to teleport 1 additional square for each essence spent.

description.value: Void is the element of the mystery. Void abilities warp space and reality, allowing you to teleport, create illusions, and make things incorporeal.

4OKrvgJOJPmbp7K9.name: Distance Is Only Memory
4OKrvgJOJPmbp7K9.description: Your elemental specialization grants you the following a feature.

3UUwF2sKeGMEdgfv.name: Mantle of Essence
3UUwF2sKeGMEdgfv.description: While you have 3 or more essence and are not dying, you exude an aura of magic whose distance is equal to your Reason score.

GgQQBhoL11UVodKm.name: One
GgQQBhoL11UVodKm.description: You become the embodiment of the element of your chosen specialization. Whenever you use magic, elemental motes flit around you and your skin changes to reflect your element, taking on an earthen or stony appearance for earth, appearing like flickering flame for fire, gaining a leaf pattern for green, and becoming a starry expanse for void. Additionally, you gain one of the following benefits, as shown on the One Specialization Features table.

effects.name: Acolyte of the Mystery
effects.description: The distance of all your abilities that have the Magic, Ranged, and Void keywords increases by 2 squares.

description.value: You use your immersion in the mystery of void magic to expand the reach of that magic better than other mages. The distance of all your abilities that have the Magic, Ranged, and Void keywords increases by 2 squares.

You instantly recognize illusions for what they are, you can see invisible creatures, and supernatural effects canâ€™t conceal creatures and objects from you. Additionally, you always know if an area or object you observe is magical or affected by magic, and you know the specifics of what that magic can do.

You also gain the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.LgMzJ9xHxs6ggP1y]{Shared Void Sense} ability.

system.story: You grant allies a taste of your unearthly vision.

effect.before: For each Victory you have, you can target one creature. Each target gains the benefit of your @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.Z6v8ZacedvuaVoy4]{A Beyonding of Vision} feature until the end of your next turn, but doesnâ€™t gain the use of the Shared Void Sense ability.

system.story: You call on the void to swallow and spit out an ally.
system.trigger: The target starts their turn, moves, or is force moved.

effect.before: You teleport the target up to a number of squares equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}. If the target moves to trigger this ability, you can teleport them at any point during the move.

spend.text: You teleport the target up to a number of squares  equal to twice your Reason score instead.

system.story: Knowledge of the mystery reveals that two spaces are the same space.

effect.before:
You open two size 1 portals in unoccupied spaces within distance, which last until you move beyond distance from any portal, end the effect as a maneuver, or are dying. Each portal must be placed at a height of no more than 1 square above the ground. When you or any ally touch a portal, that creature can choose to be instantly teleported to an unoccupied space of their choice adjacent to the other portal. If an enemy is force moved into a portal, their forced movement ends and they emerge from the other portal in an unoccupied space chosen by the creature who force moved them.

At the start of each of your turns while the portals are active, you can open a new portal connected to the others. If three or more por tals are present, you and your allies choose which portal to emerge from when entering a portal, and a creature who force moves an enemy into a portal chooses that enemyâ€™s destination portal.

description.value: Each time you finish a respite, you can open a two-way portal that leads to any place you have previously been. You and your allies can pass through the portal, which remains open for 1 hour or until you dismiss it as a main action.

While you have 3 or more essence and are not dying, you exude an aura of magic whose distance is equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}. The effects within the area of the aura are based on your specialization, as shown on the Mantle of Essence Specialization Effects table. You can activate and deactivate the aura at will (no action required).
The area provides concealment for you and your allies.

Solidity is merely a suggestion to you. Mundane barriers that are 1 square thick or less do not block your senses or line of effect. You can only sense or have line of effect past one such barrier at a time.

Additionally, whenever you use a void ability, you or one ally within distance of the ability can teleport a number of squares equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}.
A protective field of void magic absorbs violence aimed at you, then lets you hurl it back at your enemies. The first time each round that you take damage, you [[/surge 1]].

effects.name: Light Armor
effects.description: While you wear light armor, you gain a +3 bonus to Stamina, and that bonus increases by 3 at 4th, 7th, and 10th levels.

effects.name: Light Weapon
effects.description: While you wield a light weapon, you gain a +1 damage bonus with weapon abilities, including free strikes.

description.value:
You can wear light armor and wield light weapons effectively, even though you donâ€™t have a kit. While you wear light armor, you gain a +3 bonus to Stamina, and that bonus increases by 3 at 4th, 7th, and 10th levels. While you wield a light weapon, you gain a +1 damage bonus with weapon abilities, including free strikes. You can use light armor treasures and light weapon treasures.

If you have a kit, you canâ€™t take this enchantment.

You channel the substance of creation in the form of a Heroic Resource called essence, gathering and burning it to cast and maintain spells.

Essence in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain essence equal to [[lookup @hero.victories]]{your Victories}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 2 essence.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you or a creature within 10 squares takes damage that isnâ€™t untyped or holy damage, you [[/gain 1 hr]]{gain 1 essence}.

You lose any remaining essence at the end of the encounter.

Essence Outside of Combat

Though you canâ€™t gain essence outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost essence without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs essence, you canâ€™t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Victories or finish a respite.

When you use a persistent ability outside of combat (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.qOIxRq9d3diTKJzO]{Persistent Magic}), you can maintain it for a number of rounds equal to your Victories.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited essence on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of essence equal to your Victories.

Some of your heroic abilities have a persistent effect entry. For example, the Instantaneous Excavation ability has an effect noted as â€œPersistent 1.â€ Whenever you use a persistent ability, you decide whether you want to maintain it, and start doing so immediately after you first use the ability. If you maintain a persistent ability in combat, you reduce the amount of essence you earn at the start of your turn by an amount equal to the abilityâ€™s persistent value, which enables the abilityâ€™s persistent effect. All your active persistent abilities end at the end of the encounter.

You canâ€™t maintain any abilities that would make you earn a negative amount of essence at the start of your turn. You can stop maintaining an ability at any time (no action required).

If you maintain the same ability on several targets and the effect includes a power roll, you make that roll once and apply the same effect to all targets. A creature canâ€™t be affected by multiple instances of a persistent ability.

If you take damage equal to or greater than [[lookup 5*@R evaluate=true]]{5 times your Reason score} in one turn, you stop maintaining any persistent abilities. For instance, if you have a Reason score of 2 and are maintaining Instantaneous Excavation, taking 10 or more damage in one turn causes you to stop maintaining the ability.
The first time each combat round that you or a creature within 10 squares takes damage that isnâ€™t untyped or holy damage, you gain 2 essence instead of 1.

system.story: Your ally disappears, then reappears in a burst of fire.

effect.before: The target is teleported to another space within distance. Make a power roll that affects each enemy adjacent to the targetâ€™s new space.

description.value: Without needing to make a testâ€”and even without toolsâ€”you can quickly jury-rig or repair a mundane item or piece of equipment related to a skill you have from the crafting skill group. That item lasts for 1 hour or works for one use or activation (whichever comes first, as the Director determines), then breaks beyond repair. For example, if you have the Carpentry skill, you could repair a rickety wooden bridge long enough for a group of creatures to cross it, or build a simple shovel made of wood that can be used for 1 hour.
Whenever you make a test that uses the Brag, Flirt, or Intimidate skills, you can use Might instead of any other characteristic the test calls for.

system.story: You give your enemy a gentle tapâ€”like an asteroid impact.
Your curse causes your foeâ€™s flesh to rot off as spores that aid your allies.

effect.after: Choose yourself or one ally within distance. That character can end one effect on them that is ended by a saving throw or that ends at the end of their turn.

system.story: Like a stone was dropped into a pond, waves in the earth radiate from you.

effect.after: You must be touching the ground to use this ability. Additionally, you can choose a square of ground in the area that is unoccupied or is occupied by you or any ally. A pillar of earth rises out of the ground in that square, with a height in squares up to your Reason score. The pillar canâ€™t collide with any creatures or objects, nor can it force creatures raised by it to collide with other creatures or objects.

system.story: The material substance of a creature shreds away at your command

effect.before:
Until the start of your next turn, the target can move through solid matter, they ignore difficult terrain, and their movement canâ€™t provoke opportunity attacks. If the target ends their turn inside solid matter, they are forced out into the space where they entered it and this effect ends.

Persistent 1: The effect lasts until the start of your next turn.

system.story: With a flick of the wrist, you phase creatures out of existence.

effect.before: Special: The number of creatures you target with this ability is determined by your power roll.
effect.after: Each target begins to fade from existence (saveÂ ends). On their first turn while fading from existence, a target takes a bane on power rolls. At the end of their first turn, they have a double bane on power rolls. At the end of their second turn, they fade from existence for 1 hour, after which they reappear in their original space or the nearest unoccupied space.

effects.name: Combustion
effects.description: When the target ends their next turn, or if they drop to 0 Stamina before then, each enemy adjacent to them takes fire damage equal to twice your Reason score. Each affected enemy then gains this same effect [[/apply .kpvI9YZtx2l4kCrq]].

system.story: Your flames dance from kindling to kindling to kindling.

effect.after: When the target ends their next turn, or if they drop to 0 Stamina before then, each enemy adjacent to them takes [[/damage 2% fire]]{fire damage} equal to twice your Reason score. Each affected enemy then gains this same effect [[/apply kpvI9YZtx2l4kCrq]]

effect.before: Choose an enemy adjacent to you. The next ability power roll an ally who makes against that creature before the start of your next turn has an edge.

A creature who uses the Catch Breath maneuver spends a Recovery and regains [[/heal @recoveries.recoveryValue]]{Stamina} equal to their recovery value.

A creature who is dying canâ€™t use the Catch Breath maneuver, but other creatures can help them spend Recoveries in other ways.

When a creature takes the Charge main action, they move up to their speed in a straight line without jumping, then make a @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.wU69Y06G9lYFrvp6]{melee free strike} against a target when they end their move. If the creature has an ability with the Charge keyword, they can use that ability against the target instead of a free strike.

A creature canâ€™t move through difficult terrain or shift when they charge. They can fly or burrow as part of the Charge main action if they have that movement available to them, but they canâ€™t climb or swim while charging unless they can automatically use that movement at full speed.

effects.name: Defending
effects.description: When a creature takes the Defend main action, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creatureâ€™s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.

effect.before: When a creature takes the [[/apply TYggdI2Kwa302NjW]]{Defend main action}, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creatureâ€™s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.
A creature who is grabbed by another creature, an object, or an effect (see Grab below) can attempt to escape by using the following ability.
effect.after: You take a bane on this maneuver if your size is smaller than the size of the creature, object, or effect that has you grabbed.

tier2.display: You can grab the target, but if you do, the target can make a melee free strike against you before they are grabbed

tier3.display: The target is grabbed by you.

effect.before: A creature seeking to keep a foe close and locked down can attempt to grab a creature using the following ability
effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature who uses the Heal main action employs medicine or inspiring words to make an adjacent creature feel better and stay in the fight. The target creature can spend a Recovery to regain Stamina, or can make a saving throw against one effect they are suffering that is ended by a saving throw.

effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature can use the Stand Up maneuver to stand up if they are prone, ending that condition. Alternatively, they can use this maneuver to make a willing adjacent prone creature stand up.
When a creature takes the Advance move action, they move a number of squares up to their speed. They can break up this movement with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

When a creature takes the Disengage move action, they can shift [[lookup @movement.disengage]]{1} square(s). Certain class features, kits, and other rules allow a creature to shift more than 1 square when they disengage. A creature who does so can break up their shift with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

A creature can take the Ride move action only while mounted on another creature. When a creature takes the Ride move action, they cause their mount to move up to the mountâ€™s speed, taking the rider with them. Alternatively, a creature can use the Ride move action to have their mount use the Disengage move action as a free triggered action. A creature can use the Ride move action only once per round. A mounted creature can only have this move action applied to them once per round. This movement can be broken up with the riderâ€™s maneuver and main action however they wish.

biography.value: HildvarÃ° Bruneson is the DjÃºpdvergr Forgemaster of DjÃºphÃ³l â€” a 140-year-old craftsman who has maintained his forge and his people through the Fimbulwinter through sheer stubbornness and skill. He is deeply weathered, blunt, and fair. His forge still burns. He will make a deal with anyone who can help him keep it that way.
biography.director:
BEAT 17 â€” DjÃºphÃ³l (Deep-Hall), Optional Heitfolk Side Quest

He/him. DjÃºpdvergr Forgemaster. Approximately 140 years old. Deeply weathered, deeply practical. The forge chamber is 200ft across, warm, and lit by three geothermal vents. This is his domain.

THE OFFER: â€œIf you use your ability to help us repair the vent-network, I give you safe passage through Heitfolk tunnels. Emerges 12 miles north of Throat exit, on the sheltered side.â€ The tunnels bypass the worst of the Throat of World blizzard approach.

THE TASK (Lower Passages Montage, Moderate): Repair Rot-infected vent channels. 2 rounds. Kaelen spends 2 Recoveries participating (the ember-mark helps purify Rot-corruption). Possible Montage results: Total Success = 2 of 3 warmth channels restored (a year of heat); Success = 1 channel (several months); Failure = channels collapse further.

BACKGROUND (share if asked): His son died 3 years ago in a cave-in. He believes it was his fault. He does not speak of it directly but it informs everything â€” why he does not show guests the deepest forge alcove, why he gave a canteen to Kaelen specifically.

HILDVARÃâ€™S GIFT: Before the party leaves, gives Kaelen the Sealed Iron Canteen. â€œYouâ€™ll need warmth at some point that matters. Save it.â€
